title: narcissus linnaeanus subsp. italicus
intro: Narcissus linnaeanus subsp. italicus, also known as the Italian daffodil or Daffodil narcissus, is a perennial plant species in the Amaryllidaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly for its potential to treat various health conditions due to its medicinal properties, which include the presence of alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits of Narcissus linnaeanus subsp. italicus are thought to include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, fever, and cough. Herbal preparations of Narcissus linnaeanus subsp. italicus are typically made by drying the bulbs and using them in teas, infusions, or as a tincture, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using this plant for medicinal purposes.
